ksa_text: Report to board, audit committee, and executive leadership on data and AI risk posture, program maturity, regulatory compliance status, value realization, and strategic progress against roadmap commitments.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ability to interpret and incorporate data from multiple tool sources.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Leadership & Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the VP Data Governance. This task KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (vp).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Leadership & Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.457). External reviewers may question whether this specific task KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the VP Data Governance versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: McKinsey, Gartner, and Harvard Business Review consistently identify leadership and strategy skills as core differentiators for C-suite and VP data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Leadership & Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for VP Data Governance.

decision_rationale: Leadership & Strategy task KSA is within scope for the VP Data Governance at the vp tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
